vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1868: Cost-bleed circuit breaker: progress-aware, always-on guard against runaway agent spend

plan.title: Cost-bleed circuit breaker: progress-aware, always-on guard against runaway agent spend
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: An agent can silently burn unbounded cost when stuck in a loop with no forward progress. On 2026-06-13 four Kimi work agents were mis-spawned on the claude-code harness (PAN-1826 flagless-harness bug); on claude-code Kimi routes via CLIProxy and hit 'API Error: 400 ... exceeded model token limit: 262144', then sat in a /loop wakeup -> 400 -> retry cycle at 100% context. PAN-1834 burned $22.33 before a human noticed. Nothing stopped it. We need an always-on guard that makes silent cost-bleeds impossible without killing legitimately expensive deep work. The discriminator is high burn-rate + flat progress: cost is acceptable only if it buys progress.
narratives.Proposal: Add a Deacon patrol costBleedMonitor() (sibling of recoverOrphanedAgents/checkStuckAgentRemediation, inside runPatrol(), ~60s cadence) that samples each running agent's cumulative cost from the cost-events ledger (readEventsSync), tracks progress proxies (workspace HEAD-sha change + lastActivity), and applies a graduated response: WARN (high burn + flat progress -> dashboard + TTS), TRIP (sustained bleed OR a deadlock signature -> pause+stop+troubled+operator alert with diagnosis), and AUTO-REMEDIATE (harness-deadlock signature -> restart on the resolved provider-default harness). A fleet-level guard alerts (and optionally brakes) when total burn exceeds a $/hr threshold. New module family src/lib/cloister/cost-bleed*.ts mirrors the stuck-remediation layout. Thresholds live in a configurable cost_bleed block with documented defaults. The guard is idempotent and cannot self-loop (createInFlightGuard + skip-already-tripped, locked by a test).
narratives.NonGoals:
- No new CLI verb and no new dashboard UI; alerts flow through the existing activity/TTS event pipeline.
- No change to state.json.costSoFar semantics and no new runtime writer for costSoFar (it is vestigial; the cost-events ledger is the source of truth).
- No change to the existing flat-cap cost-monitor.ts / checkCostLimits visibility layer.
- No change to harness resolution or the ToS gate canUseHarnessSync.
- Remote (Fly.io) agents are skipped for the deadlock-signature tmux scan (no local pane); accurate remote-agent signature/cost handling is out of scope and documented as a limitation.
- Does not replace stuck-remediation (idle detection); cost-bleed is orthogonal (high burn, not idleness).
- Fleet auto-brake is NOT on by default (alert-only unless fleet_auto_brake is set).

autoDecisions.summary: Burn-rate cost source is the cost-events ledger (readEventsSync), not state.json.costSoFar.
autoDecisions.rationale: costSoFar is initialized to 0 at spawn and copied at handoff but has no runtime writer (verified across src/lib + src/dashboard); it is vestigial. The append-only events.jsonl ledger (src/lib/costs/events.ts) is the authoritative live per-agent cost — it captured the full $22.33 of the incident.

autoDecisions.summary: Default thresholds: warn_usd_per_min=0.50, trip_usd=5.00, no_progress_minutes=10, fleet_usd_per_hour=30, sample_window_count=10, auto_remediate=true, fleet_auto_brake=false, enabled=true.
autoDecisions.rationale: The incident burned ~$0.74/min and $22 over ~30min. Warn at $0.50/min catches it early; trip at $5 with zero progress over 10min bounds worst-case waste to a few dollars; the harness-deadlock signature trips within ~60s regardless of the dollar threshold.

autoDecisions.summary: Progress proxies = workspace HEAD-sha change + lastActivity advance; output-token/cost growth is NOT a progress proxy.
autoDecisions.rationale: A retry storm produces output tokens and cost without progress — that growth IS the bleed. Commits are the strong proxy; lastActivity is weak (retry storms advance it via tmux/jsonl heartbeats), so the deadlock-signature instant-trip backstops the case where lastActivity falsely advances.
Auto-halting the whole fleet is high-impact; alert-first respects the recoverable-vs-one-way-door principle. Operators opt into the brake.

autoDecisions.summary: New module family split into four cost-bleed*.ts files rather than one monolith.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps each bead independently reviewable and mirrors the existing stuck-remediation.ts + stuck-remediation-state.ts layout.

items.title: Add cost_bleed config block (CostBleedConfig + defaults)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/config.ts add a CostBleedConfig interface and an optional `cost_bleed?: CostBleedConfig` field on CloisterConfig (alongside `cost_limits?` at config.ts:275), and add a `cost_bleed` block to DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG (alongside the existing defaults near config.ts:300). Fields: enabled:boolean, warn_usd_per_min:number, trip_usd:number, no_progress_minutes:number, fleet_usd_per_hour:number, fleet_auto_brake:boolean, auto_remediate:boolean, sample_window_count:number. Consumers read `loadCloisterConfigSync().cost_bleed ?? DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.cost_bleed` (the same fallback pattern checkCostLimits uses at cost-monitor.ts:190). Each field carries a one-line doc comment in the codebase's style.

items.title: Given the new types, npm run typecheck passes and CloisterConfig.cost_bleed references CostBleedConfig (both exist in config.ts).

DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.cost_bleed defaults to enabled:true, warn_usd_per_min:0.5, trip_usd:5, no_progress_minutes:10, fleet_usd_per_hour:30, fleet_auto_brake:false, auto_remediate:true, sample_window_count:10.
DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.cost_bleed defaults to the documented values, verified by a deep-equal assertion in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/config.test.ts.
Create cost-bleed-state.ts per-agent ring-buffer side-state

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cloister/cost-bleed-state.ts mirroring stuck-remediation-state.ts. Persist at `${getPanopticonHome()}/agents/<id>/cost-bleed.json`. Define CostBleedSample = { ts:string; cumulativeCostUsd:number; headSha:string|null; lastActivity:string|null } and CostBleedState = { stage:'none'|'warn'|'tripped'; firstFlaggedAt:string|null; samples:CostBleedSample[]; remediation?:{ attemptedAt:string; attempts:number } }. Export readCostBleedState(agentId):CostBleedState|null, writeCostBleedState(agentId,state):void, clearCostBleedState(agentId):void, and appendSample(state,sample,maxSamples):CostBleedState (returns a new state with samples truncated to the last maxSamples, oldest dropped). Use the same fs sync helpers and try/catch-returns-null pattern as stuck-remediation-state.ts.

items.title: writeCostBleedState then readCostBleedState round-trips the persisted state; reading a missing file returns null.
appendSample(state, sample, n) returns a state whose samples array length never exceeds n, preserving the newest n samples.
clearCostBleedState removes the file; a subsequent readCostBleedState returns null.
A co-located unit test (cost-bleed-state.test.ts) covers round-trip, ring-buffer truncation, and clear against a temp HOME.
Create cost-bleed-signals.ts: cost sampling, burn rate, progress proxies

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cloister/cost-bleed-signals.ts. sampleAgentCostUsd(agentId, startedAt, readEvents = readEventsSync): number sums `.cost` of readEvents({ agentId, startDate: startedAt }) from src/lib/costs/events.ts (DECISION D1: the events ledger is the cost source, NEVER state.costSoFar). computeBurnRateUsdPerMin(samples: CostBleedSample[]): number returns (newest.cumulativeCostUsd - oldest.cumulativeCostUsd) / minutes(newest.ts - oldest.ts), returning 0 when fewer than two samples or Δt is ~0. readWorkspaceHeadSha(workspacePath): Promise<string|null> runs git via async execFile+promisify (NEVER execSync — NFR-1) ['-C', workspacePath, 'rev-parse', 'HEAD'] and returns null on error. evaluateProgress(prev: CostBleedSample, cur: CostBleedSample): boolean returns true when (cur.headSha && prev.headSha && cur.headSha !== prev.headSha) OR cur.lastActivity advanced past prev.lastActivity, else false. Inject the cost reader so tests need no real ledger.

items.title: sampleAgentCostUsd returns the summed .cost of the agent's cost events since startedAt using an injected reader, and returns 0 when the reader yields no events.
computeBurnRateUsdPerMin returns Δcost/Δminutes across the sample window and returns 0 for fewer than two samples.
evaluateProgress returns true when headSha changed, true when lastActivity advanced, and false when neither moved.
readWorkspaceHeadSha invokes git via async execFile (no execSync anywhere in the module) and returns null on error instead of throwing.
Create cost-bleed-signatures.ts deadlock-signature scanner

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cloister/cost-bleed-signatures.ts. Export detectDeadlockSignature(tail: string, ctx: { harness?: string; model: string }): { kind: 'harness-deadlock'|'context-overflow'|'rate-limit-storm'|null; diagnosis: string }. Reuse CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_PATTERNS / isContextOverflowTail from src/lib/context-overflow.ts. harness-deadlock = ctx.harness === 'claude-code' AND getProviderForModelSync(ctx.model) is NOT the anthropic provider AND the tail matches /exceeded model token limit|token limit: \d+|400[^\n]*token limit/i. rate-limit-storm = the tail contains at least K (e.g. 3) occurrences of /rate.?limit|\b429\b|retrying in/i. context-overflow = isContextOverflowTail(tail). Precedence: harness-deadlock > context-overflow > rate-limit-storm > null. diagnosis is a human-readable string naming the model, harness, and cause (e.g. 'context-limit deadlock — model kimi-k2.7-code on harness claude-code, expected provider-default pi').

items.title: Given a tail with repeated 'exceeded model token limit: 262144' and ctx { harness:'claude-code', model:'kimi-k2.7-code' }, returns kind 'harness-deadlock' with a diagnosis naming the model and harness.
Given the same tail but ctx { harness:'pi', ... } or an Anthropic model on claude-code, returns kind 'context-overflow' or null — never 'harness-deadlock'.
Given a tail with at least K '429 ... retrying in' lines and no overflow, returns kind 'rate-limit-storm'.
Given a clean tail with no signatures, returns kind null with an empty diagnosis.
Create costBleedMonitor patrol (warn/trip state machine) and wire into runPatrol

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cloister/cost-bleed.ts exporting `async function costBleedMonitor(opts?: { now?: number; config?: CostBleedConfig; agents?: AgentState[] }): Promise<string[]>`. Default now=Date.now(), config=loadCloisterConfigSync().cost_bleed ?? DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.cost_bleed, agents=listRunningAgentsSync(). If config.enabled is false, return []. For each running agent: skip remote agents (location:'remote') and agents already paused/troubled/stopped or already in stage 'tripped'. Build the current sample (cumulativeCostUsd via sampleAgentCostUsd, headSha via readWorkspaceHeadSha(workspace), lastActivity) and appendSample(state, sample, config.sample_window_count). Compute burnRate (computeBurnRateUsdPerMin) and progress (evaluateProgress vs the prior sample). Scan the tmux tail via the async capturePane(agentId, 80) and detectDeadlockSignature(tail, { harness, model }). Decide: TRIP when signature.kind !== null OR (no progress AND cost accrued since firstFlaggedAt >= config.trip_usd AND elapsed since firstFlaggedAt >= no_progress_minutes); else WARN when burnRate >= warn_usd_per_min AND !progress (set firstFlaggedAt if unset); else reset stage to 'none' and clearCostBleedState. WARN emits emitActivityEntrySync({source:'cloister',level:'warn',...}) + emitActivityTtsSync({priority:1,...}) naming agent, $/min, 'no progress'. TRIP: setAgentPausedSync(id, reason, true) + stopAgentSync(id) + markAgentTroubled(id) + emitActivityEntrySync({level:'error', details: diagnosis}) + emitActivityTtsSync({priority:0,...}); set stage 'tripped'. Guard trip side-effects per agent with a module-level createInFlightGuard() keyed by agentId. Return an action string per warn/trip. Then in deacon.ts: import costBleedMonitor and call it in runPatrol() immediately after the checkStuckAgentRemediation block (deacon.ts:5210-5212) using the existing pattern: `const costBleedActions = await costBleedMonitor(); actions.push(...costBleedActions); for (const a of costBleedActions) addLog('action', a, state.patrolCycle);`. costBleedMonitor itself is inside runPatrol so it is already gated by isDeaconGloballyPaused(). Tests inject now/config/agents and mock readEvents/git/capturePane/setAgentPausedSync/stopAgentSync/markAgentTroubled/emit* via vi.hoisted (mirror stuck-remediation.test.ts); use vi.useFakeTimers only if a real setTimeout is introduced.

items.title: Given a synthetic agent with rising cumulative cost, unchanged headSha and unchanged lastActivity across the no_progress window, the patrol pauses and stops the agent, marks it troubled, and emits an error-level activity entry + TTS naming the cause.

A synthetic agent whose headSha changes between samples is never paused or troubled, even when its burn rate exceeds warn_usd_per_min and accrued cost exceeds trip_usd (proves progress-awareness, not a flat cap).
An agent crossing warn_usd_per_min with flat progress but below trip_usd / the window emits a warn-level activity entry + TTS and is NOT paused.

Given an already-tripped (paused+troubled) agent, a second costBleedMonitor pass marks and pauses it at most once and emits no new trip side effects (idempotency spies asserted called exactly once across both passes).
runPatrol() invokes costBleedMonitor() and pushes its returned action strings into the patrol actions and addLog stream (wiring verified by reading deacon.ts).
Auto-remediate harness-deadlock: restart on resolved provider-default harness

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/cost-bleed.ts TRIP path, when config.auto_remediate is true AND the detected signature.kind === 'harness-deadlock', instead of leaving the agent in the troubled terminal state, resolve the provider-default harness via `await resolveHarness({ explicit: undefined, role: state.role, model: state.model })` (src/lib/harness-resolve.ts:62 — this enforces canUseHarnessSync, so NEVER hardcode 'pi'; NFR-4) and call `await restartAgent(agentId, { harness: resolvedHarness, message: <diagnosis + 'restarting on provider-default harness'> })` (src/lib/agents.ts:4959). Record remediation { attemptedAt, attempts } in cost-bleed-state and cap attempts at 1 (a second harness-deadlock detection for an agent that already has a remediation attempt must NOT restart again — H4 loop guard). Emit an activity entry naming old->new harness. Still pause/stop the agent before restart per restartAgent's own lifecycle; do not double-trouble after a successful remediation.

items.title: On a harness-deadlock trip with auto_remediate=true, the patrol spawns a restart (restartAgent) using the harness resolveHarness({explicit:undefined,...}) returns, and the agent is not left in the troubled terminal state.
With auto_remediate=false, a harness-deadlock trip marks the agent troubled and refuses to call restartAgent.
A second harness-deadlock detection for an agent whose cost-bleed-state already records a remediation attempt does NOT call restartAgent again (attempt cap prevents a restart loop).
restartAgent receives the harness that resolveHarness returns (verified via spy), so the ToS gate is respected and 'pi' is never hardcoded.
Fleet-level burn-rate guard (alert; optional brake)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/cost-bleed.ts add `async function evaluateFleetBurnRate(opts?: { now?: number; config?: CostBleedConfig; agents?: AgentState[] }): Promise<string[]>` that aggregates per-agent burn into a fleet USD/hour figure (sum of recent cost-events across all running agents over the trailing hour, or the sum of per-agent burn rates scaled to /hr). When the figure exceeds config.fleet_usd_per_hour, emit a global error-level emitActivityEntrySync + emitActivityTtsSync(priority:0) naming the fleet $/hr; when config.fleet_auto_brake is true, also call emergencyBrake() (src/lib/cloister/concurrency.ts:170). Add a cooldown (module-level lastFleetAlertMs vs now) so the alert fires at most once per fleet-alert window rather than every 60s. Call evaluateFleetBurnRate() as a final aggregate step inside costBleedMonitor() after the per-agent loop, concatenating its actions into the returned array.

items.title: When aggregate fleet burn exceeds fleet_usd_per_hour, an error-level activity entry + TTS naming the fleet $/hr are emitted.
With fleet_auto_brake=false (default), the guard emits the fleet alert but refuses to call emergencyBrake.
With fleet_auto_brake=true, exceeding the threshold emits the alert and applies the brake via emergencyBrake().
When aggregate fleet burn is under the threshold, neither an alert nor a brake is produced.
Document the cost-bleed circuit breaker and cost_bleed config

narrative.Action: Document the cost-bleed circuit breaker in operator-facing docs (extend the relevant cloister/cost configuration doc under docs/ and/or configuration/*.mdx). Cover: every cost_bleed field with its default and one-line meaning (a defaults table), the warn -> trip -> auto-remediate progression, the deadlock-signature instant-trip (including the harness-deadlock self-heal), the fleet $/hr guard and that it defaults to alert-only, and the statement that progress-making agents are never tripped. Docs only — no code, no tests. If a wrapper skill references the touched config doc, update it in the same commit per the skills<->CLI lockstep rule (none expected here since no CLI verb changes).

items.title: A docs page contains a defaults table that lists every cost_bleed field with its default value and one-line meaning.
The docs page contains a section covering the warn -> trip -> auto-remediate progression and stating that progress-making agents are never tripped.
The docs state that the fleet $/hr guard defaults to alert-only (fleet_auto_brake=false).
